seoTitle: How to Write on a Confluence Page (2026)
metaDescription: Write and format text in Confluence using slash commands, the floating toolbar, and team mentions, then publish once your draft is ready.
intro: Confusing layout tools and inconsistent formatting can slow down even a simple documentation update. This guide covers the basics of writing on a Confluence page — entering edit mode, using slash commands for structure, formatting text, and publishing your finished draft.

steps.heading: Enter edit mode
steps.body: Open your active space, select a target page, and click the edit pencil icon.

steps.heading: Start typing
steps.body: Click the main canvas area to begin typing, using the floating toolbar for quick text changes as you go.

steps.heading: Use slash commands for structure
steps.body: Type the / command to instantly inject headings, decisions, or bulleted list arrays into your page.

steps.heading: Apply formatting
steps.body: Highlight any paragraph to apply bolding, accent colors, or hyperlinks to relevant text.

steps.heading: Mention teammates
steps.body: Type @ followed by a name to tag a specific internal user account within your content.

steps.heading: Publish your draft
steps.body: Review your document structure and click the blue Publish button to push the changes live.

faq.question: Does Confluence auto-save my draft while I write?
faq.answer: Yes, Confluence's rendering engine auto-saves text inputs in real time to help prevent data loss across devices.

faq.question: Can two people write on the same page at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Confluence supports live multiplayer editing, so multiple people can write simultaneously without overwriting each other's paragraphs.

faq.question: How do I tag a colleague in my Confluence page?
faq.answer: Type the @ symbol followed by their account name to mention them directly in the content.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to add a heading?
faq.answer: Typing the / slash command and selecting a heading option is the quickest way to structure your content without manually formatting text.
